title: Dropdown Menu Component
content:
This file exports a set of React components that provide a customizable dropdown menu interface, built upon `@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu`. It offers a flexible and accessible way to present secondary actions or navigation options.

## Key Exports

- **`DropdownMenu`**: The root component that wraps all other dropdown menu elements.
- **`DropdownMenuTrigger`**: The element that toggles the visibility of the dropdown menu.
- **`DropdownMenuPortal`**: Portals the dropdown content to a different part of the DOM, often used for managing overflow and stacking contexts.
- **`DropdownMenuGroup`**: Groups related menu items.
- **`DropdownMenuSub`**: Creates a nested or submenu.
- **`DropdownMenuSubTrigger`**: The trigger for a submenu.
- **`DropdownMenuSubContent`**: The content container for a submenu.
- **`DropdownMenuContent`**: The main container for dropdown menu items. Accepts `sideOffset` to control its position relative to the trigger.
- **`DropdownMenuItem`**: An individual selectable item within the dropdown. Accepts `inset` for indentation.
- **`DropdownMenuCheckboxItem`**: A checkbox item that can be toggled on or off.
- **`DropdownMenuRadioItem`**: A radio item that allows for single selection within a group.
- **`DropdownMenuLabel`**: A label for a section of menu items. Accepts `inset` for indentation.
- **`DropdownMenuSeparator`**: A visual separator between menu items.
- **`DropdownMenuShortcut`**: Displays keyboard shortcuts associated with menu items.
- **`DropdownMenuRadioGroup`**: Manages the state for `DropdownMenuRadioItem` components.

## Usage Example

```tsx
import {
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
  DropdownMenuLabel,
  DropdownMenuSeparator,
} from '@/components/ui/dropdown-menu';

function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <DropdownMenu>
      <DropdownMenuTrigger>Open Menu</DropdownMenuTrigger>
      <DropdownMenuContent>
        <DropdownMenuLabel>My Account</DropdownMenuLabel>
        <DropdownMenuSeparator />
        <DropdownMenuItem>Profile</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Billing</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuItem inset>Settings</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuSeparator />
        <DropdownMenuItem>Logout</DropdownMenuItem>
      </DropdownMenuContent>
    </DropdownMenu>
  );
}
```

This example demonstrates a basic dropdown menu with a trigger, content, label, separator, and several menu items. The `inset` prop can be used to visually indent items like 'Settings'.
